name: Agent CI Build Optimization
description: Drastically reduce agent image build times from 30-45+ minutes to under 10 minutes. Current builds frequently fail due to runner timeouts and take too long to iterate on.

issues: Runtime base image is ~531MB with 60+ layers
Runners cancelled mid-build due to activeDeadlineSeconds (was 3600, now 7200)
Image pulls taking 3-4+ minutes per agent build
No pre-warming of base images on runners
GHA cache often corrupted or missing
Registry cache not being used effectively
Multiple agents competing for runner resources

goals: Zero cancelled builds due to timeouts
Consistent build times across all agents
Effective caching that survives workflow runs

userStories.title: Diagnose: Profile Current Build Times
userStories.description: Measure and document current build performance bottlenecks
acceptanceCriteria: Run gh run list --workflow agents-build.yaml --limit 20 to get recent build history
Identify which steps take longest (image pull, npm install, layer push, etc.)
Document layer sizes: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/5dlabs/runtime:latest | jq '.layers | map(.size) | add'
Identify cache hit rates from build logs
Document findings in progress.txt with specific timings

userStories.title: Optimize: Slim Runtime Base Image
userStories.description: Reduce runtime base image size by removing unnecessary tools or using multi-stage builds
acceptanceCriteria: Analyze which tools in runtime/Dockerfile are actually used by agents
Create tiered approach: runtime-minimal (essential) vs runtime-full (all tools)
Agent images use runtime-minimal by default
Runtime image size reduced by at least 30%
All existing agent functionality preserved
Document removed tools and rationale

userStories.title: Optimize: BuildKit Layer Caching
userStories.description: Maximize cache effectiveness with BuildKit optimizations
acceptanceCriteria: Enable BuildKit inline cache in all Dockerfiles: --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1
Use registry cache-from for all builds
Order Dockerfile instructions from least to most frequently changing
Verify cache hits in subsequent builds (log shows 'CACHED' for unchanged layers)
Document cache strategy in infra/images/README.md

userStories.title: Optimize: Pre-warm Base Images on Runners
userStories.description: Ensure base images are already present on runners before builds start
acceptanceCriteria: Runner image (ghcr.io/5dlabs/rust-builder) pre-pulls runtime:latest during boot
Or: Add DaemonSet that pre-warms images on all runner nodes
Build logs show 'Using cache' instead of 'Pulling' for base image
Image pull time reduced to <30 seconds
Document pre-warming strategy

userStories.title: Optimize: Parallel Build Strategy
userStories.description: Improve parallelization to reduce total pipeline time
acceptanceCriteria: Analyze runner resource usage during parallel builds
Adjust max-parallel based on runner capacity (currently 2)
Consider splitting agents into fast (dir_hash) vs slow (npm/pip) groups
Total pipeline time reduced by at least 25%
No builds fail due to resource contention

userStories.title: Optimize: npm/pip Install Caching
userStories.description: Cache package manager installations to avoid repeated downloads
acceptanceCriteria: Use BuildKit mount cache for npm: --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm
Use BuildKit mount cache for pip: --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip
Verify cached installs in build logs
Package installation time reduced by at least 50%
Cache persists across builds (check GHA cache usage)

userStories.title: Optimize: Deduplicate Common Layers
userStories.description: Share layers between agent images to reduce storage and pull times
acceptanceCriteria: Identify common layers across agent images
Ensure all agent Dockerfiles use exact same base image reference
GHCR shows shared layers between images (same SHA)
Total registry storage reduced
Subsequent agent builds reuse layers from earlier builds

userStories.title: Optimize: Reduce Layer Count
userStories.description: Combine Dockerfile RUN instructions to reduce layers
acceptanceCriteria: Audit runtime/Dockerfile for opportunities to combine RUN instructions
Reduce total layer count by at least 20%
No functionality lost from layer consolidation
Document layer optimization strategy

userStories.title: Verify: End-to-End Build Performance
userStories.description: Trigger full build and verify all optimizations are working
acceptanceCriteria: Trigger workflow_dispatch on agents-build.yaml
All agent builds complete successfully (no cancellations)
Runtime build completes in <5 min
Claude build completes in <5 min
Full pipeline completes in <15 min
Document final timings in progress.txt

userStories.title: Document: CI Build Best Practices
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update docs/ci-build-optimization.md
Document image tiering strategy
Document caching strategy (BuildKit, GHA, registry)
Document pre-warming approach
Include troubleshooting guide for slow builds

constraints: Cannot remove tools that agents actually use (verify before removing)
Must maintain backward compatibility with existing CodeRuns
Runner resources are limited (12Gi memory, 4 CPU per runner)
Must work with existing GHCR registry
